extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won the sprint after Soudal Quick-Step recce'd the final curves, exploiting barrier-side opening while Cavendish punctured late.
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step pre-race recce identified barrier-side advantage in final technical curves, avoiding congestion that boxed Kaden Groves.
Cavendish rear puncture in final 2km suggests Astana positioned too far back, unlike Evenepoel who rode front for safety.
Bennett second despite no lead-out train disruption, indicating raw speed competitive but positioning tactics inferior to Merlier's recon.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: Won second stage after deliberate course reconnaissance, demonstrating Quick-Step's tactical homework pays off in technical finishes.
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: Boxed repeatedly across multiple stages, revealing persistent positioning weakness or insufficient lead-out strength in congested finales.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Late puncture while positioned too far back suggests Astana's lead-out train unable to secure safe front positions.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Strong second without train issues but lacked recon advantage, showing speed present but tactical preparation deficit.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: GC leader positioned at front in final 2km for safety rather than hiding, tactically astute risk management.
